title: Apple opens iOS 27 public beta, putting the new Siri AI on 2.5 billion devices in preview
subtitle: The long-delayed AI Siri overhaul reached the public beta channel a year late — it now searches on-device content, follows the screen, and completes actions across apps.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Apple released the iOS 27 public beta on July 14, opening the redesigned Siri AI to anyone enrolled in Apple's Beta Software Program. The public betas for iPadOS 27, macOS 27 Golden Gate, watchOS 27, tvOS 27, and visionOS 27 shipped alongside it. The new Siri can search a user's own emails, photos, and messages, ground answers in world knowledge, respond to what is currently on the screen, hold follow-up questions in memory, and take multi-step actions across apps. The redesign was originally announced at WWDC 2025 and slipped repeatedly over the past year before landing.

The reason this matters is distribution, not capability. Apple's active device base is roughly 2.5 billion units. Even if only a fraction of users install the beta, the sample size and telemetry surface exceed what OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google combined ever get on consumer voice-assistant traffic. Siri does not have to be the smartest chatbot in the world to reshape the market — it just has to be the assistant already sitting on the phone in a customer's pocket, and it is now that assistant in a form that can plausibly do work across apps.

The competitive read is that the AI voice-assistant category is finally opening up. ChatGPT's Work Agent shipped last week, OpenAI's realtime voice stack is priced at defaults enterprises can afford, Google keeps folding Gemini into Android, and Amazon's Alexa+ has been in the field for months. The strategic vulnerability for the labs is that Apple owns the hardware and the operating system layer — if Siri gets good enough for the median user, the friction of opening a separate chatbot app disappears. The strategic vulnerability for Apple is that this is a beta of a product that shipped a year late, and reviewers who have used it describe it as 'practical but plain.'

A note for learners: pay attention to which AI features are on-device versus which round-trip to a data center, because that split determines what Apple can promise on privacy and latency. If Siri can answer a query about your emails without those emails leaving the phone, that is architecturally different from ChatGPT reading a file you uploaded. The distinction is going to matter for regulators, enterprise buyers, and eventually consumers, and Apple is betting the whole product on being the platform where that promise is credible.

sources.title: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta — TechCrunch
